page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-80 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. It features 80 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 320 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. Cache resources include 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 260 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, while single-core performance remains in the average tier. The instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, alongside top-tier performance in image processing and static web serving throughput. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4980.71 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 276.86 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to strong performance in cryptography, physics simulations, and LLM inference tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-standard-80 offers a high-density resource profile suitable for enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference. However, workloads requiring high single-threaded performance or local NVMe storage may face tradeoffs, as local storage is absent and single-core benchmark results are moderate.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-80 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs and 320 GB of RAM on an Intel x86_64 architecture. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on 40 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 260 MB L3 cache. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, particularly in image processing, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression, while single-core metrics are average. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, enabling strong cryptography, database operations, and LLM inference. This instance provides a balanced resource density, making it a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded enterprise applications, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-80 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 80 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for databases, web serving, and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-80 is an x86_64 server with 80 Intel vCPUs and 320 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance featuring advanced matrix extensions and balanced memory allocation for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 320 GB of RAM on x86_64 architecture
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput static web serving
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators
Suitable for databases, software compilation, and LLM inference
